title: Software
text:
R Packages

TRES (Tensor Regression with Envelope Structure) 
Summary: Provides three estimators for tensor response regression (TRR) and tensor predictor regression (TPR) models with tensor envelope structure. The three types of estimation approaches are generic and can be applied to any envelope estimation problems.
Reference: TRES: An R Package for Tensor Regression and Envelope Algorithms. Journal of Statistical Software, 99, 1-31.




Paper Codes

SEAS
Reference: Subspace Estimation with Automatic Dimension and Variable Selection in Sufficient Dimension Reduction. Journal of the American Statistical Association, 119(545), 343–355.


LSLDA
Reference: An Efficient Convex Formulation for Reduced-Rank Linear Discriminant Analysis in High Dimensions. Statistics Sinica, 33, 1249-1270.


GLAA
Reference: Generalized Liquid Association Analysis for Multimodal Data Integration. Journal of the American Statistical Association, 118(543), 1984–1996.


COPES
Reference: Dimension Reduction for Extreme Regression via Contour Projection. Statistics Sinica, just-accepted.

title: Jing Zeng (曾靖)
text:
Full Researcher (tenure-track)
Faculty of Business for Science and Technology
School of Management
University of Science and Technology of China

title: About me
text:
Welcome! I am a Full Researcher (tenure-track) at Faculty of Business for Science and Technology, School of Management, University of Science and Technology of China (USTC). I received my Ph.D. degree from Florida State University (FSU) in 2022 under the supervision of Professor Xin Henry Zhang and Professor Qing Mai. I received my B.S. degree from USTC in 2017.
My research interests mainly cover dimension reduction, high-dimensional statistics, robust statistics, and tensor data analysis. I am also interested in their applications in other statistical areas, e.g., extreme value theory, transfer learning, mixture model. My research is also motivated by many real applications, including finance, neuroimaging, brain imaging, multi-omics, and genomics.


Education

2017/08–2022/05: Ph.D. in Statistics, Florida State University.
2013/08–2017/06: B.S. in Mathematics and Applied Mathematics, University of Science and Technology of China.




Employment

2026/02–Present: Full Researcher (tenure-track), Faculty of Business for Science and Technology, School of Management, University of Science and Technology of China.
2022/07–2026/01: Associate Professor (tenure-track), International Institute of Finance, School of Management, University of Science and Technology of China.




Research Interests

Dimension reduction
Discriminant analysis
High-dimensional statistics
Robust statistics
Tensor data analysis
Transfer learning
Mixture model







Awards

2026/02: ‘100 Talents Project’ of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Category B
2022/07: Special Allowance for Mozi Outstanding Youth (First Prize), USTC
2022/12: R.A. Bradley Award for the best PhD dissertation in the Department of Statistics, FSU
